scope_boundary: Authority revocation propagation trace only. The trace reads already committed authority, runtime-adapter, SCIF, runtime-effect, and reference-trace artifacts and checks that over-ceiling denial, revoked authority receipt blocking, expired approval no-mutation evidence, SCIF inactive approval blocking, and blocked reference-trace authority remain visible. It does not execute a deployed authorization service, deployed revocation propagation service, runtime adapter enforcement system, approval service, sandbox, or tool wrapper.
transition_reason: The authority revocation propagation trace is useful because it connects the authority harness, runtime-adapter permission fixtures, runtime effect no-mutation controls, SCIF commit blockers, reference trace authority blockers, and finite Lean bridge into one cross-artifact denial/revocation surface. It remains repository evidence over existing artifacts, not deployed revocation behavior, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for deployed authorization enforcement, live revocation propagation, runtime adapter enforcement, approval-service quality, sandbox isolation, tool-wrapper security, model quality, benchmark performance, safety, ASI, or the System Boundaries chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable authorization-service trace
live revocation propagation trace across grant, lease, adapter, and SCIF boundaries
runtime adapter enforcement trace under revoked authority pressure
approval-service expiry and revocation behavior evidence
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: over-ceiling authority request preserves denial reason and no effect receipt
revoked authority receipt is expected to deny and block promotion
expired approval is denied before mutation with unchanged pre/post state hashes
SCIF inactive approval or unapproved destination blocks commit and dispatch
reference trace preserves missing execution authority stop condition and promotion blocker
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: over-ceiling denial record carries an effect receipt
revoked authority receipt accepted as dispatchable
SCIF inactive approval or unapproved destination dispatches a job
reference trace loses missing-authority stop condition or promotion blocker
trace described as deployed authorization, revocation propagation, adapter enforcement, approval-service, sandbox, tool-wrapper security, benchmark, safety, or support-state evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed authority-revocation claim after deployed or externally reviewable authorization-service traces, live revocation propagation across grant, lease, adapter, and SCIF boundaries, runtime adapter enforcement under revoked authority pressure, approval-service expiry/revocation behavior, sandbox or tool-wrapper enforcement review, residual accounting, independent security review, and a separate accepted upward transition are recorded.
limitations: does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent authorization, runtime-adapter, approval-service, sandbox, tool-wrapper, security, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: no deployed or externally reviewable authorization-service trace
no deployed runtime adapter enforcement trace under revoked authority pressure
no sandbox or tool-wrapper security review
non_claims: does not promote authority revocation evidence above argument

does not prove deployed authorization enforcement, deployed revocation propagation, runtime adapter enforcement, approval-service quality, sandbox isolation, tool-wrapper security, model quality, benchmark performance, safety, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the System Boundaries and Authority chapter core claim
